title: Teams search fails silently — readiness check misses upstream outage + error body lost
description: Root cause (evidence: .agency/logs/session_20260417_133517_38552): upstream MCP server agent365.svc.cloud.microsoft returned {"code":"UnexpectedError","message":"An unexpected error occurred."} during ~60s outage window. agency.exe wrapped this as HTTP 200 + JSON-RPC error response. teams-search-inline.sh readiness check (L97-105) only validated HTTP 200 → treated upstream error as 'proxy ready' → proceeded to tools/call which also failed. Additionally, mcp_search_with_retry (L138) logged generic 'empty/error' without including actual response body, making RCA impossible without digging .agency/logs/. NOTE: Initial ISS-216 blamed `head -1` SSE frame filter — this was WRONG (Step A probe confirmed agency returns single SSE frame; head -1 is benign). Fix applied: (P0) readiness check now parses SSE body, detects UnexpectedError → 10s retry → fail-fast after 3x; (P1) retry count 2→3 with exponential backoff 5s→10s; (P3) error body (first 200 chars) surfaced in log messages. Live probe also found: initialize chronically returns -32700 parse error (upstream non-standard response) but tools/call succeeds — readiness check correctly treats this as 'ready'. Repro case: 2604140040001804.
